title: Oxymeris dendroides
intro: Oxymeris dendroides, also known as the tree oyster mushroom, is a type of edible and medicinal fungus belonging to the family Tricholomataceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Oxymeris dendroides include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Oxymeris dendroides is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Oxymeris dendroides belongs to the family of corallimorphs, which are a group of marine invertebrates. Specifically, it is classified within the genus Oxymeris. This genus is further classified under the family Coralliidae. Coralliidae is a family of corals and corallimorphs. Oxymeris dendroides is known for its unique tree-like appearance.
